url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation — Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §19 + §22 + §27 + §29 + §21 house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-1 figures (FA 1998 Sch 18 para 17 £200/£400/£1000/£2000) and F-5 / Wave 7 lock (Sch 24 para 10 NO 12-month qualifier, distinct from Sch 41 para 13) carried verbatim from Stage 1 author verifications on 2026-05-27. £1m BADR cap (FA 2020 s.24 from 11 March 2020) verbatim-anchored to prevent drift to historic £10m figure.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified SI 2021/1076 regs 4/20/21/22 / TMA 1970 Sch A1 / FA (No.2) 2017 Sch 14 / FA (No.2) 2017 Sch 11 / FA 2020 Sch 2 / ITEPA 2003 Part 7A / TCGA 1992 ss.165 + 260 + 169B-169G + 169H + 169I + 169S + 169SA + Sch 7 / FA 2020 ss.23 + 24 / FA 2019 s.39 / FA 2025 Sch 5 Parts 4 + 5 / TMA 1970 ss.7 + 9A + 29 + 31A + 34 + 36 + 36A / Sch 24 FA 2007 / Sch 41 FA 2008 / SI 2018/261 / VATA 1994 Sch 10 + s.49 / FA 1998 Sch 18 para 17 / CTA 2009 s.386 / CA 2006 s.388 anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §19 + §22 + §27 + §29 + §21 locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed in M3-A-B1 D-2). Each extended brief landed at +131 to +139 lines of additional content (worked examples + 12 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). This pattern across B1 + B2 confirms that Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_RECIPROCAL_PAIR_DISCIPLINE — picks 3 (A9 hold-over-nudge) and 4 (A10 BADR-nudge) form an intentional reciprocal pair: same campaign-mechanic framing, distinct statutory regimes. Stage 2 anti-templating notes for both picks explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk and load-bearing differentiators (trade/investment line + settlor-interested trust + agricultural bifurcation for A9; rate trajectory + £1m cap + FHL transitional + 5%-throughout gate for A10). RUN session pairing of these two picks should hold the line.

D-4 STAGE2_CLUSTER_ORIENTATION_HUB — pick 6 (A12 know-about-let-property-campaign) functions as the LPC cluster orientation hub. The MW3-A LPC cluster spans 6+ pages (A2 DDS, A3 benefits, A8 loan-charge limb, A12 orientation, A14 calculator, A15 vs-no-disclosure, A17 remediation). Stage 2 anti-templating note explicitly identifies the role of this page as the definitional / process / penalty / comparative scope, distinct from decision-layer / calculator / narrative-remediation siblings. RUN session must hold this page in the orientation lane.

D-5 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 pattern (~1.5-2 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-6 STAGE2_F5_DIFFERENTIATION_CARRIED — picks 1 (A7 MTD exit), 3 (A9 hold-over), 4 (A10 BADR), 6 (A12 LPC) all depend on the F-5 / Wave 7 lock on Sch 24 para 10 (NO 12-month qualifier on unprompted floor) vs Sch 41 para 13 (12-month qualifier on equivalent floor). The Stage 1 seeds already carry F-5 alignment; Stage 2 brief extensions explicitly teach the distinction in worked examples (A12 Examples 1 + 2) and FAQ phrasing across all four picks. This site-wide discipline anchor should make the cluster pages internally consistent at RUN time.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: A7 MTD exit (vs A20 catalogue + A18 qualifying-income + A19 joint-property); A8 loan-charge (vs A2 DDS + A12 LPC orientation + close-company s.455 boundary); A9 hold-over-nudge (vs A10 BADR-nudge reciprocal pair); A10 BADR-nudge (vs A9 hold-over-nudge reciprocal pair + £1m/£10m drift catch); A11 LtdCo MTD (vs all individual-landlord MTD siblings + F-1 CT-penalty figures); A12 LPC orientation (vs A3 / A14 / A15 / A17 / A8 cluster siblings + F-5 Sch 24/Sch 41 distinction). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname differs per pick (Patel-Estate / Aldgate Developments / Aldridge-Estate / Aldridge Holiday Lets / Aldridge BTL / Aldridge-Estate again at A12 — note: A12 personas distinct from A7 personas despite overlap of Aldridge surname; sessions should treat surnames as anonymisation pool not unique-per-pick discipline).
